activities_projects.title: Structure of the Indian Judiciary
activities_projects.objective: Understand the hierarchy of courts in India
steps: Draw a diagram showing Supreme Court, High Court and District Court
Write one function of each court
Explain why this hierarchy is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the judicial hierarchy in India

activities_projects.title: Independence of the Judiciary
activities_projects.objective: Understand why the judiciary must be independent
steps: Write what judicial independence means
List two reasons why courts must be independent
Explain how it helps citizens
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the importance of judicial independence

activities_projects.title: Types of Cases in Courts
activities_projects.objective: Learn about different types of legal cases
steps: Write the meaning of civil and criminal cases
Give one example of each type
Explain which court may hear these cases
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands types of cases handled by courts

activities_projects.title: Public Interest Litigation Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of Public Interest Litigation (PIL)
steps: Write what Public Interest Litigation means
Explain why PIL is important for people
Give one example of a situation where PIL can be filed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how PIL helps protect public rights

activities_projects.title: Role of Courts in Protecting Rights
activities_projects.objective: Understand how courts protect Fundamental Rights
steps: Write two Fundamental Rights
Explain how courts protect these rights
Give a short example
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises the judiciary’s role in protecting rights

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Solving a Dispute
steps: Write a short situation involving a dispute
Explain how the case would reach the court
Explain how the judge might solve it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the process of dispute resolution

activities_projects.title: Legal Vocabulary List
activities_projects.objective: Learn key terms used in the judiciary
steps: Write words like appeal, verdict, witness, evidence
Write their meanings
Use one word in a sentence

activities_projects.title: Importance of Evidence
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of evidence in court cases
steps: Write what evidence means in a court case
List two types of evidence
Explain why evidence is important for justice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how evidence helps courts make decisions

activities_projects.title: Courtroom Roles
activities_projects.objective: Understand the roles of different people in a courtroom
steps: Write the roles of judge, lawyer, and witness
Explain how each helps in a court case
Give an example situation

activities_projects.title: Discussion: Justice for All
activities_projects.objective: Encourage thinking about fairness in society
steps: Discuss why courts are important in a democracy
Write two points from the discussion
Write a short summary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of justice and democracy
